title: alnus subcordata var. villosa
intro: Alnus subcordata var. villosa, also known as the smooth alder, is a variety of alder tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The smooth alder has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used to create herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Alnus subcordata var. villosa include its ability to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
